retry_reason: kref_put always succeeds; if the refcount reaches zero the object is freed. Retrying would constitute a double-put leading to use-after-free.
contract_summary: This is a put wrapper: it releases a reference to a chtls_sock object. When the reference count reaches zero, the release function chtls_sock_free is called to clean up the object. After calling __chtls_sock_put, the caller must not use the csk pointer unless it holds another valid reference. The 'fn' parameter is intended for debugging/tracing and does not affect the refcount semantics. Callers must have previously acquired a reference (e.g., via a get operation) before calling this function.
